_metadata.title: Operation Epic Fury — Day-by-Day OSINT Timeline
_metadata.note: Auto-generated backfill. Values marked null are unconfirmed. Verify against primary sources.

sources: militarytimes.com - Six dead, 18 service members injured in Iran operation
jpost.com - Two more US servicemembers killed in Iran
military.com - Three US service members killed, several injured Operation Epic Fury
cibercuba.com - US reveals names of four soldiers killed

YCharts - WTI Crude Oil Spot Price (Historical Data)
Barchart - Crude Oil Prices Soar as Strait of Hormuz Closed to Tanker Traffic

key_operations: Task Force Scorpion Strike (one-way attack drones)

casualties.notes: Initial assault casualties not separately reported for Day 1. By March 1-2, cumulative US KIA reached 3-6 and WIA reached 5-18. Iranian Red Crescent reported 555 total Iranian deaths by mid-day EST on February 28 (initial assault).
casualties.confidence_explanation: Day 1 (Feb 28) casualty figures are not explicitly broken out in search results. Available data conflates initial assault casualties with subsequent days' totals. Only Iranian civilian estimate (555) is directly attributed to the initial assault timeframe.

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: 9 naval craft destroyed within hours of initial attack on Feb 28, including Iranian drone carrier Shahid Bagheri. CENTCOM later claimed all 11 ships in Gulf of Oman destroyed, but verification incomplete as of reporting date.
notes.us_ships_damaged: No US naval vessel damage reported in first 24 hours. Iranian ballistic missile and Shahed drone struck near US 5th Fleet NSA facility in Bahrain (200m from HMS Juffair) but no direct US ship hits documented.
notes.hormuz_status: Strait remained open Feb 28 with maritime warnings issued. Iran's IRGC called for closure but no successful blockade implemented on Day 1.
notes.mines_detected: No mine detection data reported in available sources for Feb 28, 2026.

notes.brent_oil_usd: Interpolated from Feb 27 (66.96) and Feb 26 (65.10) WTI prices; Brent typically trades 6-7 USD/bbl above WTI[5]
notes.wti_oil_usd: Feb 27, 2026 closing price of 66.96 USD/bbl used as proxy for Feb 28[5]
notes.hormuz_flow_pct: Not reported in search results; Strait of Hormuz closure confirmed as of March 3, 2026[1]
notes.shipping_disruption: Confirmed: Barchart headline states 'Crude Oil Prices Soar as Strait of Hormuz Closed to Tanker Traffic' dated March 3, 2026[1]
notes.confidence_rationale: WTI price data is reliable but Feb 28 specific data unavailable (using Feb 27 proxy). Hormuz closure timing and shipping disruption percentage unconfirmed in provided sources.

notes.iran_missiles_fired_context: Iran launched at least 162 missiles at Israel on March 1, with a sharp decline from 58 missiles in late evening Feb 28 to 29 in late evening to 12 by March 1 afternoon
notes.intercept_data_limitation: Search results confirm CENTCOM intercepted 'hundreds of Iranian missile and drone attacks' but do not provide specific numbers for March 1 alone
notes.damage_limitation: No specific damage figures from Iranian missile strikes reported in search results for March 1

economic.notes: Search results do not contain specific data for March 1, 2026. The earliest available WTI data in results is March 2, 2026 (71.13 USD/barrel). No data found for Brent on March 1, 2026. Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic and shipping disruption metrics are not present in provided search results. Operation Epic Fury context cannot be verified from commodity price data alone.

casualties.notes: US KIA confirmed at 6 by Secretary Hegseth and General Caine briefing on March 4, 2026[5]. US WIA (seriously wounded) reported as 5 from initial Iranian attacks on March 1-2[1][2]. Iran military and civilian casualty figures not provided in available sources as of March 4.

naval.hormuz_status: IRGC claimed complete control, but no Iranian ships underway per US statements; effectively closed due to risks

missiles.damage_from_missiles: Damage to Al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar; extent undisclosed. No casualties reported.

air.notes: Only one-way attack drone shots down 73% from opening days is confirmed from available search results. Specific aircraft destroyed counts, IADS sites neutralized, and US aircraft losses are not reported in the provided search results as of March 5, 2026. Search results contain data through March 4, 2026 briefings but lack granular March 5 specific metrics.

proxy.notes: Search results contain information through March 5, 2026, but do not provide specific quantified data for Hezbollah rocket launches, Houthi attacks, Iraqi militia attacks, or proxy casualty figures for March 5 specifically. Available data shows: March 2 (several projectiles), March 3 (approximately 30 rockets per Ynet), March 4 (drone and missile launches). No Houthi or Iraqi militia attack data found in search results.
proxy.confidence_explanation: Search results lack specific operational data for March 5, 2026. Only Hezbollah-Israel front is documented; Houthi and Iraqi militia activity absent from provided sources.

cyber_diplomatic.note: The provided search results contain no specific information about cyber incidents, diplomatic developments, UN actions, or ceasefire talks on March 6, 2026. Search results focus on casualty figures, military operations, and strategic objectives as of late March 2026, but do not include granular daily reporting for March 6.

sources: armyrecognition.com - US Surpasses 6,000 Combat Sorties in Operation Epic Fury

key_operations: US surpassed 6,000 combat sorties by March 7
Intensified strikes on Iran's missile, drone, and naval forces
military_ops.notes: Search results lack specific Day 8 (March 7) metrics. Only sorties figure is available from a source referencing March 7 operations. Specific strike counts and target numbers for this date are not reported in provided search results. Later updates show 8,000+ targets struck by Day 22 (March 21) and 10,000+ by Week 4, but granular Day 8 data is unavailable.

air.notes: Specific metrics for March 7, 2026 are not available in search results. Available data points to earlier dates: By March 5, over 1,000 targets struck in first 72 hours; by March 25, over 10,000 targets struck total with two-thirds of Iran's missile/drone/naval production facilities destroyed. Admiral Cooper reported on March 5 that ballistic missile attacks decreased 90% and drone attacks decreased 83% since day one.
air.reason_for_null_values: Search results do not contain granular daily breakdowns for March 7, 2026 specifically. Data is aggregated across multi-day periods or provided for other dates within the operation.

proxy.notes: On March 7, 2026, Hezbollah conducted extensive rocket and missile attacks on northern Israel. The IDF reported that a Hezbollah projectile near the Lebanese border caused injuries to 8 Israeli soldiers, with 5 sustaining severe injuries, including the son of Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich. Israel responded with airstrikes on Hezbollah headquarters in Beirut's southern suburbs and military sites in southern and eastern Lebanon. Houthi and Iraqi militia activity for this specific date is not reported in available sources.

cyber_diplomatic.note: The provided search results contain no specific information about cyber incidents, diplomatic developments, UN actions, or ceasefire talks on March 8, 2026. The search results focus on military casualty figures (as of March 24-25), operation objectives, and general campaign updates from early March, but do not include detailed daily reporting for March 8 specifically.

key_operations: Ongoing air campaign against Iranian regime targets
Joint US-Israel operations (Epic Fury and Roaring Lion)
Iranian retaliatory strikes (Operation True Promise IV)
military_ops.notes: Search results do not contain specific strike counts, target numbers, or sortie data for March 9, 2026. The JINSA source references a March 9 update but the actual content is not provided in search results. Specific operational metrics are only available for later dates (March 13 and April 1). Confidence is low due to absence of March 9-specific data.

casualties.notes: As of April 5, 2026, the most recent confirmed US casualty figure is 7 KIA and 9 seriously wounded. Wikipedia reports 15 total US soldiers killed by end of conflict, but specific March 9 breakdown unavailable. Iranian military and civilian casualty estimates not provided in search results for this specific date.
casualties.data_limitations: Search results provide cumulative casualty figures through early April 2026, not specific daily breakdown for March 9. US KIA/WIA figures are confirmed as of April 5. Iranian military and civilian casualty estimates not disaggregated by date in provided sources.

missiles.notes: On March 9, Iran fired 35 missiles, representing a 26 percent decline from March 8. The UAE detected 18 UAVs and 15 ballistic missiles fired from Iran on March 9. Specific intercept rates, leaker counts, and damage assessments for March 9 are not provided in available sources.

naval.hormuz_status: 16 mine-laying vessels destroyed; strategic strait threatened but under US control

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: CENTCOM reported over 50 Iranian naval vessels destroyed as of March 9; Trump stated total reached more than 30 mine-laying vessels by March 16[1][4]
notes.us_ships_damaged: No US naval vessel damage reported in search results for this date
notes.hormuz_status: As of March 10, 16 mine-laying vessels threatening the Strait of Hormuz had been destroyed[1]
notes.mines_detected: Search results reference mine-laying vessels destroyed but do not specify total mines detected

economic.notes: Brent crude price on March 10, 2026 estimated at approximately $90/barrel based on video timeline showing $90 price point on March 9-10. WTI price not reported in search results for this date. Hormuz flow percentage and shipping disruption metrics not provided in available sources. High uncertainty due to limited specific data for March 10.

notes.hezbollah_rockets_fired: Hezbollah launched approximately 200 rockets toward northern Israel in a joint Iran-Hezbollah attack. Of these, 80 fell short of the border and landed in Lebanon. This was described as Hezbollah's largest barrage since the November 2024 ceasefire.
notes.houthi_attacks: No data found in search results for March 10, 2026
notes.iraqi_militia_attacks: No data found in search results for March 10, 2026
notes.proxy_casualties: Search results do not provide specific casualty figures for March 10. General reports indicate minimal Israeli casualties from the barrage, with no fatalities reported by Magen David Adom, though several people were injured moving to protected spaces.

proxy.confidence_notes: High confidence on Hezbollah rocket count (200) from multiple corroborating sources. No data available for Houthi or Iraqi militia activity on this specific date. Casualty data incomplete for this specific date.

casualties.notes: No specific casualty figures available for March 11, 2026. Search results provide cumulative data as of March 2, March 8, March 10, and March 24, but no discrete daily breakdown for March 11.
casualties.reason: Search results do not contain casualty data specifically for March 11, 2026. Daily granularity is not provided in available sources.

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: Handala executed destructive wiper attack on Stryker Corporation, wiping over 200,000 devices using Microsoft Intune; pre-positioned backdoors by MuddyWater on US bank, airport, defense firm; cyberattacks on IRGC-linked banks.[1][2][3]

air.notes: Specific metrics for March 12, 2026 (Day 13) are not available in search results. Available data provides cumulative figures as of March 25-26 (Days 26-27): CENTCOM reported destroying over 10,000 targets total, 92% of large Iranian Navy ships, and approximately 20 US aircraft damaged/destroyed in the first month. Individual daily breakdowns for March 12 are not disclosed in provided sources.

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: Handala-linked malware attack disrupted US medical device manufacturer Stryker's Microsoft environment on March 11; Poland foiled cyberattack on national nuclear center with possible Iranian links[3][5].

military_ops.key_operations: Strikes targeting Iran's ballistic missile and drone capabilities, Iranian Navy (particularly sea mine laying capabilities), and capacity to attack commercial vessels; drone strikes on Basij militia positions in Tehran (noted from March 11 actions); U.S. helicopter Hellfire missile strike on Iranian vessel approaching USS Abraham Lincoln; loss of U.S. KC-135 over Iraq with four confirmed deceased crew members

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: No specific cyber incidents reported on March 13, 2026. Ongoing Handala campaign against Stryker (initiated March 11) and general escalation in cyber offensives noted for March 12-13 period, with focus shifting to enterprise and financial targets[1][3][4].

naval.hormuz_status: Threatened but passage impeded; US strikes on Kharg Island, Trump threatens oil infrastructure if Iran continues impeding ships

military_ops.key_operations: Precision strike on Kharg Island destroying naval mine storage, missile bunkers, and 90+ military targets

missiles.notes: Iran fired 19 ballistic missiles on March 15, 2026[2]. Of these, 9 successfully hit targets (leakers)[2], while 4 ballistic missiles and 6 UAVs were engaged by UAE air defenses on March 15[7], suggesting at least 10 projectiles were intercepted or engaged. The 'damage_from_missiles' field is not specified in available sources. Confidence is moderate due to incomplete intercept data across all defensive systems.

economic.note: Search results contain only March 11, 2026 data (Brent: $90.96/barrel) and general May futures information. No specific data available for March 15, 2026. Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic and disruption metrics are not included in provided search results.

proxy.notes: Search results contain limited specific data for March 15, 2026. Result [5] references March 15, 2026 as entering the war's third week with ongoing missile and rocket fire from Iran and Lebanon, but does not provide quantified attack figures for that specific date. Results [1], [3], and [4] cover March 12, 26, and early March respectively, but lack precise March 15 metrics. No Houthi or Iraqi militia attack data appears in provided search results.

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: Pro-Iranian hacker groups and Iranian cyber units expanded cyber operations against Israel, Gulf states, and U.S. interests, targeting government platforms, energy companies, and critical infrastructure on 15 March 2026[1]

missiles.damage_from_missiles: 3 hits on UAE, 1 hit on unspecified target
missiles.notes: Iran fired 14 ballistic missiles on March 16[1]. Successfully hitting targets decreased to 4 hits total, with 3 striking the UAE[1]. UAE air defense engaged 6 ballistic missiles and 21 UAVs on March 16[8]. Specific intercept count not reported in available sources; 'leakers' represents confirmed successful hits rather than failed intercepts.

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: Pro-Iranian hacker groups and cyber units expanded operations against Israel, Gulf states, and U.S. interests, targeting government platforms, energy companies, and critical infrastructure. Tenable analysis highlights a Microsoft Word N-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-21514) affecting 14 million assets as a key threat[2][3].

military_ops.key_operations: Israeli Air Force (IAF) strikes on gas facilities at South Pars Special Economic Energy Zone, coordinated with US

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: Secretary Hegseth confirmed over 120 Iranian navy ships neutralized as of March 19 update; Admiral Cooper reported over 100 destroyed by March 16, with 30+ ships sunk by March 18
notes.us_ships_damaged: USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier experienced fire on board, expected to temporarily dock in Crete
notes.hormuz_status: US forces confirmed Iran's naval capability in Gulf of Oman neutralized; 16 mine-laying vessels destroyed as of March 10, total reached 30+ by March 16
notes.mines_detected: No specific mine detection count reported for March 18; 16 mine-laying vessels destroyed by March 10

naval.confidence_notes: Ship destruction figures vary across sources (100-120 range); USS Gerald R. Ford damage confirmed; Hormuz status and mine-laying vessel counts well-documented; specific mine detection count for March 18 not available in search results

notes.iads_sites_neutralized: Search results do not contain specific IADS sites neutralized on March 18, 2026
notes.us_aircraft_lost: Search results do not contain specific US aircraft losses on March 18, 2026. General operation data shows 7 total US aircraft lost by late March and over a dozen MQ-9 Reapers destroyed throughout the operation

economic.notes: Brent price of $108.78/barrel confirmed for March 18, 2026 at 9:15 AM ET[1]. WTI price not reported in search results for this specific date. Strait of Hormuz shipping disruption confirmed as occurring during this period[4], but specific percentage flow data unavailable. Confidence reduced due to missing WTI data and lack of quantified shipping metrics.

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: CISA issued an emergency advisory on March 18 regarding a novel TTP from the Handala Stryker attack, weaponizing Microsoft Intune to wipe over 200,000 devices, bypassing EDR/antivirus[1].

casualties.notes: Search results provide cumulative casualty data through early April 2026, but do not contain specific casualty figures for March 19, 2026. The most recent dated report (March 1) indicates 3 US KIA and 5 US WIA, with cumulative totals reaching 7 US KIA and 9 US WIA by early April. Iranian casualty estimates are not provided in search results.

air.notes: Search results contain aggregate data through March 23, 2026, but do not provide granular daily breakdowns for March 20 specifically. Available data shows: (1) By March 23, U.S. conducted 10,000+ combat flights with strikes against 10,000+ Iranian targets[4]; (2) By March 19 (Day 20), IDF struck 130+ drones, missile launchers, and air defense systems in Iran[2]; (3) Overall Operation Epic Fury: 13 U.S. service members killed, 300+ wounded, ~20 U.S. aircraft damaged[1][7]; (4) Initial strikes targeted 1,000+ targets including air defenses and IRGC facilities[4].
air.confidence_explanation: Daily-specific metrics for March 20 are not reported in available search results. Data is aggregated across operation phases or reported through later dates. High uncertainty due to lack of granular daily reporting.

missiles.notes: Only UAE air defense engagement data available for March 21. UAE systems engaged 3 ballistic missiles and 8 UAVs. Total Iranian missiles fired and leaker counts for this specific date not reported in available sources.

cyber_incidents.ongoing_campaigns: AI-powered information warfare with 110+ deepfake images and videos detected; FBI alert on March 20 regarding Iranian malware distribution via Telegram

military_ops.key_operations: Israeli strikes targeted several regime sites throughout Iran, including anti-aircraft missile sites

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: Admiral Cooper stated 'we've struck over 8,000 military targets, including 130 Iranian vessels' as of March 21-22, 2026. Other sources cite 120-140+ vessels destroyed through March 23.
notes.us_ships_damaged: Not reported in available sources for this specific date
notes.hormuz_status: Iran disrupted maritime traffic near Strait of Hormuz in response to Operation Epic Fury
notes.mines_detected: 44 mine layers identified and engaged per Secretary Hegseth statements

military_ops.key_operations: IDF widespread strikes in Tehran on government locations and infrastructure including regime safe houses; strikes on IRGC Al-Ghadir Corps HQ in Yazd; strikes on Iranian State Broadcasting Center transmitter in Bandar Abbas; unattributed strike on PMF base in Al Anbar, Iraq; unclaimed strike on PMF fighters in Kirkuk, Iraq

casualties.notes: As of March 24 (Day 25), CENTCOM reported 290 US wounded with 13 KIA. The March 23 figure is interpolated from the March 24 report. Iran casualty figures from HRANA (March 21) estimated 3,230 total killed including 1,400+ civilians; Iranian military breakdown not separately reported. KC-135 crash (6 crew) occurred March 20-21, contributing to KIA total.

sources: gulfif.org - Operation Epic Fury and the Collapse of Iran's Layered Naval Defense

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: As of March 24, 2026, U.S. forces had destroyed or incapacitated more than 120 Iranian naval vessels across both IRIN and IRGCN[3]. Earlier reports cited 30+ ships destroyed, but the March 24 source indicates the cumulative total reached over 120 by that date[8].
notes.us_ships_damaged: No U.S. naval vessel damage reported in available sources for this date.
notes.hormuz_status: Iran disrupted maritime traffic near the Strait of Hormuz on this major global shipping route[7]. Trump administration officials expected it would take several weeks to reopen the strait in a potential future U.S. operation[5].
notes.mines_detected: No specific mine detection data reported for March 24, 2026 in available sources.
notes.us_casualties: As of March 24, 2026: 13 killed, 290 wounded (255 returned to duty, 10 seriously wounded)[7]
notes.targets_struck: Over 9,000 military targets destroyed inside Iran by March 24[8]

cyber_diplomatic.cyber_incidents: Pay2Key deployed ransomware against a second US healthcare institution[1]

key_operations: Israeli Air Force struck several key weapons production sites in Tehran, including two long-range naval cruise missile manufacturing facilities and several other aerial and naval weapons production sites for supplying Hamas, Hezbollah, and Iran’s other proxies
Unattributed strike on a base shared by Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) and Iraqi Armed Forces in Al Anbar Governorate, killing seven Iraqi soldiers and wounding 13 others
US strikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq continued
US Air Force A-10 Thunderbolt II used to strike Iranian naval vessels

naval.hormuz_status: US invasion of Larak Island to counter threats to ships in Strait of Hormuz; maritime traffic previously disrupted by Iran

economic.notes: Brent price confirmed at $105.85/barrel as of 9 a.m. ET on March 26, 2026[2]. WTI price not reported in search results. Hormuz flow estimated at 0% based on Trump's stated refusal to reopen the Strait and Iran's lack of concrete reopening protocol[4]. Shipping disruption confirmed as ongoing with no resolution plan offered[4]. Confidence reduced due to missing WTI data and indirect inference of Hormuz flow status.

notes.us_kia_source: Pentagon confirmed 13 American servicemen killed as of March 26, 2026[5]
notes.us_wia_source: CNN reported approximately 290 American servicemen injured as of Tuesday (March 25), representing 200 more than the previous week[5]
notes.iran_military_killed_est_note: Iranian Armed Forces claimed 600-800 US killed (disputed); no official US estimate of Iranian military casualties found in search results
notes.civilian_casualties_note: Initial reports (March 1-2) indicated 201+ killed and 700+ injured across Iran per Iranian Red Crescent; no March 27 specific data available

casualties.confidence_rationale: US casualty figures (KIA/WIA) are based on official Pentagon/CENTCOM statements through March 26. No specific March 27 data available in search results. Iranian casualty claims are unverified Iranian government statements. Civilian casualty data is from early March only.

missiles.notes: Search results confirm 20 ballistic missiles and 37 UAVs were engaged on March 28, 2026, but do not specify intercept rates, leaker counts, or damage assessments for that specific date. The 'engaged' figure indicates U.S. defensive action but does not clarify how many were successfully intercepted versus leaked through defenses.

economic.shipping_disruption: 17.8 million barrels per day transit disrupted
economic.notes: Brent and WTI prices confirmed from March 28, 2026 reporting. Hormuz flow percentage not reported in search results; disruption magnitude (17.8 million barrels/day) cited as estimated transit disruption from Iran's Hormuz yuan-toll mechanism. Confidence reduced due to lack of specific percentage flow data and reliance on single primary source for price figures.

cyber_diplomatic.note: The provided search results contain detailed information about Operation Epic Fury from February 28 through mid-March 2026, with the latest specific date referenced being March 25, 2026 (UAE air defense engagement). No search results contain information about March 28, 2026 specifically. The search results do not include data on diplomatic developments, UN actions, or ceasefire talks for any date in the operation timeline. To provide accurate JSON data for March 28, 2026, search results with information from that specific date would be required.

notes.iran_ships_destroyed: As of March 19, 2026, over 120 Iranian naval vessels had been destroyed or incapacitated across IRIN and IRGCN. By March 30, this figure likely remained consistent or higher, but no specific update for that date was found in search results.
notes.us_ships_damaged: No U.S. naval vessel damage reported in available sources.
notes.hormuz_status: U.S. forces established total air and sea dominance. As of March 10, 16 mine-laying vessels threatening the Strait of Hormuz had been destroyed.
notes.mines_detected: 16 mine-laying vessels destroyed as of March 10, 2026. No additional mine detection data available for March 30.

naval.confidence_notes: High confidence on ship destruction figures (120+) based on multiple CENTCOM sources through mid-March. Lower confidence for March 30 specifically, as search results do not contain updates from that exact date. Most recent confirmed data is from March 19, 2026.

notes.wti_oil_usd: Monthly rise of 48% reported[3] but specific March 30 price not provided in search results
notes.hormuz_flow_pct: No quantitative flow percentage data available; however, search results indicate the Strait remained disrupted
notes.shipping_disruption: Confirmed: President Trump pledged escalation against Iran with no concrete plans to reopen Strait of Hormuz[5]. Iran was drafting a protocol with Oman to monitor traffic, indicating continued closure[5]

notes.iran_aircraft_destroyed: No specific count for March 31 available. Sources indicate Iran's air force has been largely destroyed overall, but day-specific figures not provided.
notes.iads_sites_neutralized: No specific count for March 31 available. CENTCOM reports air defense systems 'largely been destroyed' but precise March 31 figures not disclosed.
notes.us_aircraft_lost: One E-3 Sentry AWACS aircraft destroyed on ground in Saudi Arabia on March 31 in Iranian strike. Several KC-135 tankers also damaged.
notes.air_superiority_pct: Admiral Cooper stated 'we don't see their aircraft flying' and air defense systems 'largely been destroyed,' but no quantified percentage provided for March 31.

proxy.notes: Hezbollah rocket count aggregated from multiple barrages reported on March 31: 45 rockets + 20+ rockets + 15 rockets = 80 total. Israeli wounded figure includes 3 lightly injured in Western Galilee impact and 1 moderately wounded man in northern Israel. Houthi and Iraqi militia activity not reported in available sources for this date. Hezbollah casualty figures not specified in search results.
